title: OECD: US Growth to Slow to 1.6% in 2025 Amid Trade War Fallout
body: OECD forecasts U.S. growth will slow to 1.6% in 2025 as Trump’s tariffs raise costs and dampen global trade and business confidence.

title: May Deflation Signals Weak Consumer Demand, Industry Struggles in Indonesia
body:
Indonesia posts 0.37% deflation in May, with weak demand and limited stimulus raising fears of deeper slowdown and rising layoffs.

title: Minister Bahlil: Energy Sector to Create 6.2M Jobs by 2030
body: Indonesia may create 6.2M direct jobs by 2030 in power, mining, and EV sectors, with a major push for green and sustainable occupations.

title: US Still Indonesia's Biggest Surplus Driver as Tariff Talk Deadline Nears
body: The US remains Indonesia’s biggest trade surplus driver, contributing about $5.44 billion to Jakarta's positive trade balance until April.

title: Indonesian Palm Oil Export Value Jumps 20 Pct, But Volume Drops
body: Indonesia exported crude palm oil (CPO) and its derivatives worth a total of $7.05 billion in January-April 2025.

title: EU Readies ‘Countermeasures’ If Tariff Deal with US Falls Apart
body: The EU prepares countermeasures after Trump slaps 50% steel tariffs, threatening to derail ongoing trade talks with Brussels.

title: BPS: Indonesia Records 0.37% Deflation in May, Led by Cheaper Chilies and Garlic
body: Indonesia recorded a 0.37% deflation in May, driven by falling food prices, including garlic and chili, as imports decreased by over 14% year-over-year.

body: Cumulatively, Indonesia booked a $11.07 billion trade surplus from January to April 2025.

title: You Don’t Need to Be Young, Tall, or Attractive to Get Hired Anymore in Indonesia
